title: Le temple enseveli
summaries: "Le temple enseveli" by Maurice Maeterlinck is a philosophical treatise written in the early 20th century. The work delves into the nature of justice, questioning its foundations and exploring the relationship between human morality and natural law. The text grapples with the ideas of physical and social justice, emphasizing the complexities and often the contradictions inherent in human actions and societal norms.  At the start of the book, the author presents a profound discourse on justice for those who do not believe in an omniscient Judge overseeing moral behavior. He raises critical questions about the existence of justice beyond human laws and societal judgments, encouraging the reader to reflect on the essence of justice as related to social dynamics rather than divine oversight. The opening sets the stage for an in-depth examination of various forms of justice, including physical, psychological, and social, and the inherent challenges in understanding and attaining true justice amidst human fallibility.
